Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We are given a process tree that is underfitting and allows unwanted behavior The current model does SEQUENCE Create Purchase Requisition Approve Purchase Requisition PARALLEL Create Purchase Order Receive Goods Receive Invoice Match Invoice to PO Pay Invoice Close Case This parallel block allows any order of the inner activities which is not correct

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The intended order is 1 Create Purchase Requisition 2 Approve Purchase Requisition 3 Create Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Receive Invoice 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Pay Invoice 8 Close Case However note that the model also allows activities to occur in any order within the parallel block which breaks the sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Steps that must be sequential and cannot be parallel Create Purchase Order must come after the approval and before the receiving of goods and invoice

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Receiving goods and receiving invoice might be independent But note typically you cannot receive goods without having the purchase order Also you cannot match an invoice until you have both the goods and the invoice

Name: Validation - PE
Text: But note the problem states the standard logic as 3 Create Purchase Order 4 Receive Goods 5 Receive Invoice 6 Match Invoice to PO 7 Pay Invoice So we must enforce that order

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The issues with the current model The parallel block allows any order For example it allows Creating the PO after receiving goods Not allowed Paying the invoice before matching Not allowed

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Proposed fixed model We must break the parallel block into a sequence

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: The sequence should be create req approve req create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - IND
Text: In reality the invoice might be received before the goods But the problem states the order So we stick to the order receive goods then receive invoice

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Therefore a strict sequence of 8 steps is acceptable

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: root equals ProcessTree operator equals Operator SEQUENCE children equals create req approve req create po receive goods receive invoice match invoice pay invoice close case

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This will enforce create po must occur after approve req and before receive goods receive goods must occur after create po and before receive invoice This prevents Paying the invoice before matching Paying before receiving goods Creating the PO after receiving goods

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Explanation The updated process tree is a strict sequence of the 8 activities This ensures that The purchase requisition is created first then approved Then the purchase order is created Then the goods are received Then the invoice is received Then the invoice is matched to the purchase order and to the goods receipt Then the invoice is paid Finally the case is closed

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This sequence prevents any out of order execution For example It is impossible to create the purchase order before the requisition is approved because the sequence requires the approval to come before the PO creation It is impossible to receive goods before the purchase order is created because the PO creation comes before the goods receipt It is impossible to pay the invoice before it is matched because the matching comes before payment It is impossible to close the case before payment because the payment comes before closing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: This model is more constrained than the original and only allows the intended order
